rationale: Both candidates correctly validate the already-fixed qwen3_tts scoping and independently rediscover the same async_chunk default-value inconsistency (True vs False) that echoes the ground-truth Low comment, with concrete file/line callouts. Neither addresses amy-why's 'should this live in the model' question, but Y goes further by flagging that the split now keys solely on total_num_scheduled_tokens and dropped the prior hidden-shape alignment, correctly noting main later generalized to_payload_element to match either axis — this is precisely the dual-batch-axis gap that PR #4910 fixed and that human reviewers missed. X's extra findings (NPU runner parity, a missing test assertion) are grounded but more tangential and don't hit the specific latent gap. Y's insight is a genuine differentiator in review depth even though both are comparable in overall precision and actionability.
